digest-mcp-consolidation-infrastructure-.explanation: The MCP consolidation and infrastructure paradox is a concrete present-tense instance of the Bainbridge pattern — capability (MCP deployment velocity) expanding faster than governance infrastructure.
The invisible cost stack analysis (intent specification, alignment monitoring, governance overhead) maps directly to the DCFB constitutional design requirements — making them concrete and costed.

research-bainbridge-warning.explanation: The Bainbridge Warning is the applied diagnostic operationalisation of DCFB — translating the five constitutional principles into a gap analysis framework for institutional deployment.
This research paper is the theoretical foundation for the Bainbridge Warning product offering — the product operationalises the research as a delivered assessment service.
The Bainbridge Warning gap analysis depends on the irreversibility account of trust — Bainbridge zones are structurally defined by where irreversibility accumulates faster than governance.
Both operate in domains [governance] and the content has direct governance relevance.

research-dcfb.explanation: DCFB provides the five constitutional principles that the Bainbridge Warning diagnostic operationalises as a capability-governance gap analysis.
DCFB Principle 1 (field-level assessment) directly motivates the intent specification requirement — you cannot specify intent at the node level in a distributed field.
DCFB Principle 4 (distributed monitoring) requires knowing when deployment decisions cross irreversibility thresholds — the governance trigger the clause formalises.

research-trust-irreversibility-residue.explanation: The irreversibility account of trust builds directly on DCFB Principle 4 (distributed monitoring) — governance calibrated to the actual distribution of irreversibility in the field.
This research directly produced the Irreversibility Threshold Review Requirement clause — the clause text appears verbatim as the 'Clause Candidate' at the end of this piece.
The irreversibility account clarifies the structural mechanism behind Bainbridge failures — capability-governance gaps are irreversibility gaps, not just confidence gaps.
Both operate in domains [governance, epistemics] and the content has direct governance relevance.

product-clearbid.explanation: ClearBid applies the intent specification principle from CIR to the procurement domain — closing the gap between what organisations intend to specify and what they receive.

product-martha.explanation: Martha delivers the CIR framework as a team-applied cohort experience — it takes the self-assessment instrument and builds it into a structured group learning process.
Martha is a practical example of DCFB applied at team scale — the cohort structure is designed around the field, not individual participants.
